public int removePalindromeSub(String s) {
    int n = s.length();
    for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; i++) {
        if (s.charAt(i) != s.charAt(n - 1 - i)) {
            return 2;
        }
    }
    return 1;
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int removePalindromeSub(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; i++) {
            if (s.charAt(i) != s.charAt(n - 1 - i)) {
                return 2;
            }
        }
        return 1;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution leverages the fact that the input string contains only 'a' and 'b'. If the string is already a palindrome, it can be removed in one step. Otherwise, it takes at most two steps by removing all 'a's then 'b's (or vice versa). The algorithm checks for palindrome in O(n) time with O(1) space.
